content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reconfigured to dynamically gate which environment transitions are stored in episodic memory based on the surprise signal generated by curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods like ICM and RND, rather than labeling all novel transitions for active learning uniformly. This gating would prioritize active learning annotation and long-term memory storage only for high-surprise transitions that actually improve the agent’s generalization, cutting redundant memory usage and boosting sample efficiency.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
